module: Prototype Layer Validation — GNN / NSA / ACO+Kuramoto
dataset_used: Quartz WLTP 36-cell pack (10 parquet files, 634,450 rows)

criterion_A_chronic.metric: mean voltage deficit from pack mean across all 634,450 rows
criterion_A_chronic.mechanism: chronic undervoltage — higher internal resistance or position-dependent bias; both S12 cells at end-of-string
criterion_A_chronic.prior_session_claim: P3S10 weakest — reconciled: prior session used min-instantaneous criterion, not mean deficit

criterion_B_transient.metric: minimum instantaneous voltage in SOC<5% subset (15,292 rows, all 36 cells present)
criterion_B_transient.mechanism: capacity exhaustion — cell depletes first at deep discharge (lower capacity than peers)

rank_correlation_between_criteria.interpretation: The two criteria are statistically independent — they measure different failure modes. Confirmed: rs=-0.01 ≈ orthogonal.

recalibration.approach: Option (a) — 2D effective subspace (V_norm, T_norm) only; 6D→2D scope reduction documented in code docstring
recalibration.root_cause_fixed: Original failure: hand-crafted 6D fault centroids at min-distance 0.70 from any real observation, r=0.25, 2.8× gap. Root cause: 4/6 features (SOC, SOH, SEI_norm, plating_risk) are imputed constants identical across all cells — the 6D self-cloud is a 2D slice; centroids placed in the remaining 6D space are unreachable by real data.
recalibration.2d_scope_limitation_restated: The recalibrated NSA operates only on V_norm and T_norm. It cannot detect SOH-based faults, SEI growth, or plating risk — those features are imputed constants with zero inter-cell information. This limitation does NOT disappear with recalibration; it is a hardware/data constraint that persists until real per-cell SOH/SEI/plating sensors exist.

stage_1_self_cloud.self_set_definition: 35 per-cell mean (V_norm, T_norm) positions across all 634,450 WLTP timesteps
stage_1_self_cloud.fringe_cells_are_thermal: Cells furthest from centroid (P1S1, P3S4, P1S7) are thermal position outliers (end-of-string T_norm extremes), not voltage/capacity outliers

pre_registration_deviation.deviation: Self-set used in commit 1eb8ff2 was 35 per-cell MEAN positions, not real per-timestep readings as pre-registration specified ('real healthy readings sampled uniformly across all cells AND TIMESTEPS').
pre_registration_deviation.why_taken: Stage 1 analysis surfaced two interpretations of 'p95 self-cloud radius' (0.035 per-cell-means vs 0.311 all-observations). The pre-registration rationale mentioned '~0.025–0.056 normalized units', which matches the per-cell-means span. This was an interpretation error — the pre-registration text is unambiguous. Deviation was flagged by user review of commit 1eb8ff2.
pre_registration_deviation.impact_on_conclusion: Per-cell-means run produced degenerate non-zero scores (PRIMARY std=0.000158) with T_norm-position-driven residual structure. Timestep-self-set rerun produces PRIMARY std=0.000000 (all exactly zero) — a stronger null. The final null verdict does NOT change, but the mechanism is now cleaner: Type A (cycling envelope covers Quartz operating range), Type B and Type C both ruled out. The per-cell-means LOCO (1 point of 35) had no statistical power; retracted and replaced by the timestep LOCO below (2,884 points removed per cell).

stage_2_detector_training_means_run._note: DEVIATED FROM PRE-REGISTRATION — documented above. Results retained for audit trail.

stage_2_detector_training.self_set: 100,940 per-timestep (V_norm, T_norm) observations — every 220th row × 35 cells (PRE-REGISTERED)
stage_2_detector_training.r_formula: 0.4 × p95 — single pre-chosen multiplier, not tuned
stage_2_detector_training.pre_score_prediction: By construction, any survivor is > r from all self-points. The nearest Quartz observations to the survivor boundary are exactly at the self-cloud p95 boundary, which is r away from the nearest survivor. No activation is possible before scoring begins.

stage_3_scores.scoring_primary: mean over all 634,450 timesteps of per-timestep activation fraction
stage_3_scores.scoring_secondary: max over all 634,450 timesteps of per-timestep activation fraction

secondary_range.max_cells: P3S4 (0.000242), P3S9 (0.000110), P1S1 (0.000022) — extreme T outliers, single-timestep activations

stage_3_scores.degeneracy_verdict: DEGENERATE — PRIMARY all exactly 0.0; SECONDARY std=0.000071 < 0.001
stage_3_scores.null_type_A_confirmed: Cycling-envelope self-set at r=0.124 covers the entire Quartz WLTP operating range. Surviving detectors exist only in T/V extremes no cell ever reaches: T_norm > 0.466 (>48°C, Quartz max 40°C), T_norm < -0.203 (<14.9°C), V_norm outside [0, 0.930]. The 47× cycling/inter-cell ratio (V_norm cycling std=0.188 vs inter-cell differences=0.004) is confirmed as the structural explanation — not an artifact of the per-cell-means shortcut.
stage_3_scores.secondary_trace_explanation: Three cells show non-zero secondary scores (P3S4=0.000242, P3S9=0.000110, P1S1=0.000022). These are single-timestep activations where those cells' T_norm reaches within r of the detector boundary at the high-T fringe. All are below any physically meaningful threshold and contribute std=0.000071, well below the 0.001 degeneracy threshold.

stage_4_spearman_2x2.note: PRIMARY scores are all exactly 0 — Spearman undefined for primary. SECONDARY scores have std=0.000071; correlation analysis would operate on effectively constant values. Table is trivially NULL; not re-run on all-zero primary.
stage_4_spearman_2x2.2x2_summary: ALL NULL — primary scores all-zero; secondary scores degenerate (std=0.000071)

loco_diagnostic.self_set_type: timestep self-set (corrected), 100,940 self-points; removed 2,884 readings per held-out cell (2.9%)
loco_diagnostic.retraction: Previous LOCO (commit 1eb8ff2) removed 1 of 35 mean points — statistical power was negligible. 'Type C ruled out definitively' claim from that run is RETRACTED and replaced by this structural argument.
loco_diagnostic.conclusion: In this geometry, single-cell circularity is structurally impossible. At r=0.124 the entire Quartz operating range is covered by the 100,940-point self-set; removing any single cell's 2,884 readings (2.9%) cannot open a gap larger than the V_norm offset between that cell and its neighbours (~0.004), which is negligible relative to r. The surviving detector count is identical (45,386) before and after removal for both P3S12 and P3S10. Type C is ruled out by entailment — no individual cell defines any part of the self-boundary — not by an empirical test with independent statistical power.

nsa_weakest_cell.final_result: DEGENERATE NULL — Null Type A confirmed. PRIMARY exactly 0 for all 35 cells; SECONDARY marginally non-zero but degenerate (std=0.000071 < 0.001, three thermal-fringe cells with single-timestep activations). The cycling envelope of a healthy WLTP pack occupies the entire NSA operating region at r=0.124; no non-self region is reachable by any real Quartz observation. Type C ruled out by entailment (structural impossibility, not empirical LOCO power).
nsa_weakest_cell.2d_scope_limitation_final: Restated per pre-registration requirement: the recalibrated NSA operates on V_norm × T_norm only. No SOH, SEI, or plating sensitivity is possible without real per-cell sensors. For relative health ranking within a healthy homogeneous pack, the appropriate tool is a pack-centroid distance metric (Mahalanobis or similar). NSA is designed for binary fault/non-fault detection with degraded cells in the training set — structurally unsuitable for fine-grained health ranking within a healthy population.
nsa_weakest_cell.code_updated: diagnosis/weakest_cell.py rewritten (2D, genuine negative selection, 6D expansion path). All 20 validate() tests PASS.

vdetector_arc.unit_tests: 14/14 PASS — self-deletion correct (r_i <= nearest_dist for all detectors); array interface matches scalar; saturation check operational

1_standard_nsa_fixed_r.method: Fixed-r NSA, r=0.124 (0.4 × p95), timestep self-set, 100,940 points
1_standard_nsa_fixed_r.result: DEGENERATE NULL — 97.7% of detectors outside Quartz operating envelope; PRIMARY exactly 0.000000 for all 35 cells

2_cross_cell_rank.method: V-detector δ=3× positive control, 12-cell synthetic pack, seed=42
2_cross_cell_rank.result: CONFOUNDED — P1S7 ranks top-1 at δ=0 (score=0.000073) with no injection; natural cell-to-cell variation exceeds injected signal; Stage 1 SUCCESS re-characterised as confounded

3_loo_within_cell_delta.method: 35-cell LOO within-cell delta, both injection modes, seeds 42+43
3_loo_within_cell_delta.result: UNDERPOWERED — gate not yet applied; Mode A 30/35 positive but median(delta_A)=4.43e-7 vs noise_ceiling=9.92e-7 (2-seed estimate)

4_power_gate_4seed.method: 4-seed power gate (seeds 42–45), per-cell max-pairwise noise floor
4_power_gate_4seed.result: GATE FAILS — ratio=0.32 (threshold >=2). noise_ceiling=1.372e-6, median(delta_A)=4.429e-7. Test underpowered: comparing two noise sources, not signal vs noise.
4_power_gate_4seed.root_cause: 92.8% of detectors capped at r_max=0.150 (far from self-boundary); only 7.2% (~3,500 of 48,800) are near-boundary and geometrically capable of sensing the δ=0.012 injection. Effective boundary-sensitive detector count insufficient to resolve signal above sampling noise.

vdetector_arc.conclusion: In the healthy Quartz pack, weak-cell detection in V/T space is not achievable by any NSA variant because the injected or natural signal (delta=0.012, inter-cell spread=0.004) is 16–47x smaller than the cycling amplitude (V_norm std=0.188). This is a data/geometry limit, independent of algorithm. V-detector calibration is sound (14/14 unit tests, self-deletion correct, saturation check clear); it simply has no signal to resolve here. The Null Type A geometry is confirmed by four independent methods, all yielding the same structural message.
vdetector_arc.r_max_tuning_warning: DO NOT tune r_max to force ratio above 2. If r_max sensitivity is explored it must be a pre-registered study reporting the full ratio-vs-r_max curve, not a single winning value. Even resolving a SYNTHETIC injection would not constitute proof of real weak-cell detection in a genuinely degraded pack — it would only confirm algorithm mechanics on manufactured signal. Any claim of real detection requires a dataset with genuine degraded-cell signal and an independent fault ground truth.
vdetector_arc.mode_b_attribution_preregistered: Mode B effective mean offset at WLTP V_norm≈0.55: delta×(1-0.55)=0.0054 — 2.2× weaker than Mode A (0.0120). Mode B fail is ambiguous between (a) algorithm shape-blind and (b) weaker effective signal. Attribution requires equal-signal comparison: Mode A at delta=0.005 vs Mode B at delta=0.012. Deferred until power gate passes on a dataset with genuine signal.
vdetector_arc.next_stage_justification: BattGP (Zenodo 13715694) provides 28 fault-return LFP packs with per-cell voltage time series (U_Cell_1..8) and real degraded cells. Power-gate protocol applies before any detection verdict.

battgp_vdetector_preregistration.status: FULLY LOCKED — all conditions set before 27-system batch run. Commits: 34d096f (initial), b89c635 (GP-confidence gate), 12668d0 (pipeline+smoke test), pending (trivial verdict rule + calibration note).

calibration_system.why_burned: Bound-setting required identifying the healthy 7 cells (by ranking p50 voltages, U_Cell_3 lowest) — this looked at which cell is faulty. Any detection result on data_sys_17 is contaminated by this snooping. Detection on data_sys_17 is shown as secondary/sanity only, never as primary evidence.
calibration_system.held_out_systems: all 28 systems except data_sys_17 (27 systems) form the primary held-out validation set

feature_engineering.v_norm_derivation: healthy-7 cells of data_sys_17 (U_Cell_1,2,4,5,6,7,8) under BattGP segment criteria
feature_engineering.why_healthy_bounds_not_all_cells: All-8 version is circular: U_Cell_3's 2.567 V min sets V_low, accommodating exactly the cell we aim to detect. Healthy-7 anchors normalization to the normal operating range.
feature_engineering.t_norm_assignment: per BattGP TEMP_MAP: cells 1-2 -> T_Cell_1_2, cells 3-4 -> T_Cell_3_4, cells 5-6 -> T_Cell_5_6, cells 7-8 -> T_Cell_7_8

high_current_subsegment.threshold_derivation: median |I_Batt| in data_sys_17 discharge segments = 14.6 A, rounded to 15 A
high_current_subsegment.signal_at_threshold: V gap healthy-vs-degraded = 232 mV (delta_V_norm = 0.955) vs 67 mV (0.276) full-segment — 3.5x improvement
high_current_subsegment.re_derive_rule: Once first 5 held-out systems are loaded, recompute pooled median |I| across calibration + those 5 systems. If pooled median deviates from 15 A by more than 5 A, update threshold and freeze it for all remaining systems. If within 5 A, keep 15 A. This re-derivation must complete BEFORE any detection is run on remaining systems.

detection_protocol.method: LOO within each system — 8 iterations per system

per_iteration.self_set: pool high-current (V_norm, T_norm) features from the other 7 cells
per_iteration.primary_score_i: mean activation fraction of cell i's high-current features

detection_protocol.vdetector_prediction: cell with highest PRIMARY score = V-detector predicted weak cell
detection_protocol.r_max_warning: r_max is NOT tuned. Any r_max sensitivity study must report the full ratio-vs-r_max curve, not a single winning value.

trivial_baseline.definition: trivial_score_i = mean(V_norm_i) on high-current sub-segment samples; trivial_weak_cell = argmin(trivial_score_i)
trivial_baseline.implementation: one-line mean — no training, no detectors, no hyperparameters
trivial_baseline.pre_registered_comparator_rule: V-detector is reported as adding value ONLY IF its agreement rate with GP-inferred weak cell >= trivial baseline agreement rate. If V-detector merely replicates per-cell-mean ranking, this is reported honestly: the algorithm adds nothing over the mean on this data.
trivial_baseline.primary_metric: agreement fraction with gp_pred, reported separately for trivial and V-detector

power_gate.signal_per_seed: gap = max(PRIMARY_i) - mean(PRIMARY_j for j != argmax)
power_gate.noise_ceiling: p95 of {per-cell max-pairwise |delta gap| across 4 seeds}
power_gate.gate_fail_is_first_class_result: gate-fail count (X/27) reported as primary result, not hidden

baseline_outcomes: BASELINE-AGREE: gate passes AND trivial top-1 == GP
BASELINE-DISAGREE: gate passes AND trivial top-1 != GP
outcomes_per_held_out_system.gp_source: BattGP calc_fault_probabilities output — independent of our pipeline
outcomes_per_held_out_system.framing: method-agreement study, not validation against independent ground truth
four_mutually_exclusive: AGREE: power gate passes AND GP-confident AND V-detector top-1 == GP-inferred weak cell
DISAGREE: power gate passes AND GP-confident AND V-detector top-1 != GP-inferred weak cell
GATE-FAIL: power gate ratio < 2 (detection underpowered; no prediction made)
NO-CLEAR-FAULT: power gate passes AND GP does not flag any cell above r0_upper_threshold=2.0e-3 Ohm (pack may be healthy or fault is below GP detection threshold; AGREE/DISAGREE not evaluated)
outcomes_per_held_out_system.baseline_outcomes_note: Trivial baseline outcomes use the same GP-confidence precondition: BASELINE-AGREE and BASELINE-DISAGREE are evaluated only when gate passes AND GP-confident. Trivial score is computed on all gate-pass systems regardless, but agreement only counted on GP-confident subset.

battgp_vdetector_preregistration.mode_b_attribution: DEFERRED — at constant high current the Mode A vs Mode B distinction is near-absent. Deferred until a study with broader current range or synthetic injection.

data_sys_17_snooped_sanity_check.label: CALIBRATION SYSTEM — snooped, excluded from primary results
data_sys_17_snooped_sanity_check.known_answer: U_Cell_3 degraded (p50=3.218V vs healthy p50=3.285V; min=2.567V vs healthy min=3.116V)

gp_confidence_gating.rationale: All 28 systems are warranty returns, but README states returns can be driven by BMS warnings, user judgment, or support advice — not necessarily a cell fault. On a pack with no genuinely weak cell, both GP and V-detector pick a top-1 by noise; AGREE/DISAGREE is then meaningless.
gp_confidence_gating.gp_source: BattGP calc_fault_probabilities with causal=True, r0_band=0.55e-3, r0_upper_threshold=2.0e-3 (used only for threshold fault prob column, not used for GP-confidence gate — see correction below)
gp_confidence_gating.gp_confident_definition: GP-confident = max(R_upper{i} band_i fault prob, over last 24 hourly GP predictions, across cells i=1..8) >= 0.5. R_upper{i} band_i fault prob is P(R0_i > mean_pack_without_i + r0_band=0.55e-3 Ohm), the relative upper-band probability computed by BattGP's calc_fault_probabilities. This is the correct metric: absolute r0_upper_threshold=2.0e-3 is UNUSABLE here because all cells in this pack have baseline R0 of 8-10 mOhm (pack age: 2013-2016), well above the 2 mOhm absolute line. Band-relative probability is independent of pack baseline resistance and correctly isolates relative outliers.
gp_confidence_gating.no_clear_fault_definition: max(R_upper{i} band_i fault prob, last 24 timesteps) < 0.5. Pack is excluded from AGREE/DISAGREE; reported as NO-CLEAR-FAULT count.
gp_confidence_gating.evaluation_preconditions: AGREE or DISAGREE are evaluated ONLY when BOTH (a) power gate passes (ratio >= 2) AND (b) GP flags at least one cell above r0_upper_threshold.
full_breakdown_reported: N_gate_pass    : systems where power gate ratio >= 2
N_gp_confident : gate-pass systems where GP flags >= 1 cell above threshold
N_agree        : GP-confident AND V-detector top-1 == GP weak cell
N_disagree     : GP-confident AND V-detector top-1 != GP weak cell
N_no_fault     : gate-pass systems where GP flags no cell above threshold
N_gate_fail    : systems where power gate ratio < 2
gp_confidence_gating.no_silent_drops: Every held-out system appears in exactly one of: N_agree + N_disagree + N_no_fault + N_gate_fail = N_held_out. No pack is silently excluded.
gp_confidence_gating.gp_weak_cell_definition: argmax(mean R_upper{i} band_i fault prob over last 24 hourly predictions), evaluated only when GP-confident=True

smoke_test_calibration_note.why_this_is_NOT_evidence: data_sys_17 is the CALIBRATION system: V_norm bounds were set from its healthy-7 cells, and Cell 3 (the fault) was identified during bound-setting. AGREE on this system is EXPECTED by construction — Cell 3 has a severe fault (R0=17.2 mOhm vs pack mean 8.7 mOhm, ~2x elevation), the V_norm formula was anchored to exactly this pack's healthy cells, and the high-current threshold was derived from this system's discharge profile. Ratio=13.38 on a 2x-R0 fault with snooped normalization is not a predictive baseline for held-out results.
smoke_test_calibration_note.held_out_interpretation: The 27 held-out systems are the real test. Their faults may be far more subtle than Cell 3 in data_sys_17. A lower held-out AGREE-rate is a real finding, not a regression relative to the smoke test. The smoke test AGREE establishes only that the pipeline is wired correctly end-to-end — nothing about detection power on novel systems. Held-out results are interpreted on their own terms, independent of the smoke test numbers.

trivial_vs_vdetector_verdict_rule.counting_on: gate-pass AND GP-confident held-out systems only (the N_gp_confident subset)

required_counts.N_same: count where trivial_pred == vdetector_pred (V-detector is redundant on these — identical prediction, no added value possible)
required_counts.N_different: count where trivial_pred != vdetector_pred (the ONLY cases where V-detector can demonstrate value over the baseline)

trivial_vs_vdetector_verdict_rule.value_test: In the N_different subset ONLY: count how many times V-detector agrees with GP vs how many times trivial agrees with GP. This is the sole evidentiary test of whether the immune-inspired algorithm beats the one-line median. If N_different is 0 or very small, V-detector has no opportunity to demonstrate value.
trivial_vs_vdetector_verdict_rule.headline_rule: The 7-field breakdown (N_total / N_gate_pass / N_gp_confident / N_agree / N_disagree / N_no_fault / N_gate_fail) MUST be reported alongside N_same and N_different. Reporting a headline agreement rate without this split is not permitted.

pre_registered_honest_conclusion.trigger: trivial_pred == vdetector_pred on >= 90% of GP-confident gate-pass systems
pre_registered_honest_conclusion.mandatory_wording: On BattGP LFP field data, the V-detector reduces to per-cell mean-voltage ranking; the negative-selection machinery adds no detection value over a one-line baseline. The immune-inspired approach is not justified on this data.
pre_registered_honest_conclusion.rationale: Locked pre-batch to eliminate post-hoc incentive to rationalize V-detector value. If the trivial baseline and V-detector make identical predictions, the sophisticated algorithm is not contributing — and that is the honest result to report.

gp_input_cap.rationale: Original run thrashed on sys_8 (3.6GB) and sys_6 (2.9GB) — GP inference at full resolution is O(n^2) in timesteps. 40,000 rows (≈5,000/cell) leaves sys_1-4 unchanged (<35k rows), caps sys_5+ aggressively. Applied identically to ALL systems.
gp_input_cap.locked_commit: pending — set before any new run on sys_7-28
gp_input_cap.validation_required: Must re-run sys_1 with capped GP and confirm GP weak cell still = 7 with band_prob >= 0.5 before launching remaining systems.

spot_audit_cap.description: Post-hoc full-data spot audit on 3 lowest-band_prob GP-confident systems
spot_audit_cap.rationale: The 40k cap is fast but could dilute a near-boundary system. Running the 3 worst cases at full data provides a severity-range coverage check without re-running everything.
spot_audit_cap.selection_criterion: GP-confident systems (outcome AGREE or DISAGREE) sorted ascending by max_band_prob; top 3 where cap actually bound (n_gp_rows < n_seg_rows)
spot_audit_cap.verdict_rule: If any DIFFERS: report both capped and full-data conclusions; flag which outcome depends on the capped run. If all MATCH: cap integrity confirmed across the severity range.
spot_audit_cap.locked_mid_batch: sys_7 completed (AGREE); sys_8 in progress at time of lock; no result reviewed that could influence selection criterion

hc_input_cap.description: HC-row cap on V-detector LOO and trivial baseline: stride-subsample df_hc to 20,000 rows per cell before power gate
hc_input_cap.rationale: sys_8 had 287,734 HC rows; uncapped LOO trains on 2M+ stacked rows per seed-cell iteration, consuming 63+ CPU-minutes with zero seeds complete. Cap applied identically to all systems. V-detector is a distance-based ranker — temporal density adds no signal beyond shape distribution.
validation_required_before_batch: sys_4: HC-capped V-detector must still pick Cell 1 (subtle case where V-detector beat trivial baseline)
sys_7: HC-capped V-detector must still agree with GP (AGREE, GP weak cell=5)
hc_input_cap.fallback: If sys_4 V-detector edge vanishes under 20k cap, raise to 50k and re-validate

validation_result.reason: Only completed system where cap genuinely removed data (170,519 → 21,315 HC rows, stride=8, 87.5% removed)
validation_result.note_on_sys4_sys7: Both had <20k HC rows (4,645 and 5,719); cap never fired; those runs confirmed no regression on small systems but did not test cap subsampling

residual_validation_gap.description: HC-cap validated on sys_6 (moderate/severe fault, 170k HC rows). NOT validated on a system that is BOTH subtle-fault (severity_ratio < 1.3) AND large-HC (>20k HC rows pre-cap). No such system exists among sys_1-7 — the subtle cases (sys_4: severity=1.152, sys_7: severity=1.163) had only 4,645 and 5,719 HC rows respectively.
residual_validation_gap.spot_check_rule: If any remaining system (sys_8-28) has severity_ratio < 1.3 AND n_hc_raw > 20000, re-run that system at 80k HC cap vs 20k HC cap and compare before trusting its outcome.

value_test_on_N_different.net: V-detector beats trivial in 2 of 3 N_different cases

hc_spotcheck_sys8.trigger: sys_8: severity=1.072 (< 1.3) AND n_hc_raw=287,734 (> 60k) — residual validation gap rule
hc_spotcheck_sys8.interpretation: HC-cap subsampling did not affect V-detector outcome on the subtle+large-HC system

gp_cap_spot_audit.method: stride = max(1, n_seg // 40000); cap cuts data only when stride > 1 (n_seg >= 80k)

corrected_breakdown_under_full_gp.note: sys_8 changes AGREE->DISAGREE(V=trivial=6, GP=3). N_same/N_different unchanged since sys_8 has V=trivial throughout.

revised_outcome_under_full_data_gp.impact_on_N_different: No change — sys_8 remains V=trivial, so N_different stays 3
revised_outcome_under_full_data_gp.impact_on_N_agree: Drops from 9 to 8; N_disagree rises from 3 to 4
revised_outcome_under_full_data_gp.note: Official registered-protocol outcome is AGREE (40k cap used uniformly). Spot-audit revised outcome is provided for transparency only.

authoritative_final_counts.basis: full-data GP for all systems (sys_8 corrected)
authoritative_final_counts.value_test: V wins: sys_4 (Cell1 vs trivial=8), sys_9 (Cell2 vs trivial=8). Trivial wins: sys_20 (trivial=8=GP, V=2).

check_1_KCL.description: ACO outputs must sum to I_total across 50 real-parameter draws

check_2_monotone.description: Best cell (SOH=0.95, risk=0.05) gets more current than worst (SOH=0.75, risk=0.40)
check_2_monotone.calibration_concern: In 8 of 10 scenarios the worst cell receives exactly 0.000A — full current concentration on best cell, not modulation. With I_total=2A split across 3 cells, the heuristic eta differential (0.95/0.05=19 vs 0.75/0.40=1.875) is so large that all 20 ants route to the best cell. Real packs require minimum current floors to prevent cell starvation.

check_3_kuramoto_convergence.description: Order parameter R increases after 50 Kuramoto steps on real-param SOC imbalance
check_3_kuramoto_convergence.calibration_concern: delta_soc magnitudes reach ±13–17% SOC per call (mean_abs=9.9%). This exceeds physically realizable balancing rates for passive or active cell balancers — a typical balancer achieves <1% SOC correction per cycle. The Kuramoto output should be treated as a synchronization target direction, not a directly actionable setpoint.

check_4_TR_risk.description: TR risk = 0 for all Quartz temperatures; correct boundary behavior

boundary_checks.at_onset_80C: tr_risk=0.0 (correct — onset is not yet runaway)

checks.output_bounds: PASS — Sigmoid head enforces [0,1]; min=0.394 max=0.740

real_distribution_input_test.finding: std≈0.000 across all 20 output nodes for real Quartz inputs (mean std=0.000819 vs 0.043327 on random-wide inputs). GNN produces near-identical outputs for all cells when inputs are near-uniform (SOC=0.99 for all, V spread=37mV, T spread=2.8°C).

root_cause_investigation.experiment: Repeated with BatchNorm replaced by identity (same random-weight seed, same real inputs). Result: std=0.000819 — IDENTICAL to the BatchNorm case. Layer-by-layer probe: BN changes std from 0.003359 to 0.003359 (no change) because in eval mode with default init (running_mean=0, running_var=1), BatchNorm1d acts as identity.
root_cause_investigation.conclusion: RANDOM-WEIGHT ARTIFACT, NOT BATCHNORM. Collapse persists when BN is disabled. The collapse arises because random weight matrices W map near-identical input vectors to near-identical output vectors: ||W·(x_i−x_j)|| ≤ ||W||·||x_i−x_j||, and with Quartz's tiny inter-cell spread (0.025 normalized units in V_norm), even typical-scale W produces near-zero inter-node differences. BatchNorm is not a contributing factor.

real_distribution_input_test.implication: The near-zero std is a random-weight artifact on near-uniform inputs — not an architectural property that would persist after training. Whether trained weights would learn to amplify small inter-cell differences (and achieve per-cell discrimination) cannot be assessed without training data. This is consistent with the broader 'accuracy-untestable' status of the GNN.

data_limitation.n_nodes_mismatch: GNN hardcoded to N_NODES=20; Quartz has 36 cells. Architecture change required.
data_limitation.no_supervised_target: No per-cell SOC or SOH ground truth exists in Quartz dataset. Accuracy cannot be measured — only architecture correctness.
data_limitation.statement: GNN validated for architecture correctness only. Accuracy validation requires: (a) dataset with per-cell SOC/SOH ground truth, OR (b) synthetic training data from DFN cell model. This is a DATA LIMITATION, not a model limitation.

gnn_graphsage.overall: ALL ARCHITECTURE CHECKS PASS — accuracy validation not possible with available data

NSA_recalibrated.category: NSA — recalibrated correctly (genuine negative selection, 2D V/T subspace, pre-registered protocol); outcome = Null Type A confirmed by four independent methods (fixed-r NSA, cross-cell rank confound, LOO within-cell delta underpowered, 4-seed power gate ratio=0.32). Root cause: cycling amplitude (0.188) dominates inter-cell spread (0.004) and δ=3× injection (0.012) by 47× and 16× respectively. V-detector calibration sound (14/14 unit tests); geometry structurally limits detection. Recommended replacement: pack-centroid relative outlier score (validated against criterion B only — see final_project_report.md §6.1). Next stage: BattGP (28 fault-return packs, genuine degraded-cell signal, power-gate protocol pre-registered).

ACO_Check_2_mono.detail: calibration concern: worst cell starved to 0A

ACO_Check_3_Kura.detail: R: 0.739→0.995; calibration concern: delta_soc up to ±17%

ACO_Check_4_TR.detail: all Quartz temps well below 80°C onset

GNN_architecture.detail: accuracy validation not possible; near-zero inter-node std on uniform inputs is random-weight artifact (not BatchNorm — confirmed by BN-disabled control, std identical)
